content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the MongoDB StatefulSet and replica set in the cluster.

input.command: kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o yaml | head -100

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-replica","namespace":"mongodb"},"spec":{"replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"serviceName":"mongodb-replica-svc","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["mongod","--replSet","mongodb-replica","--bind_ip_all","--auth","--keyFile=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile"],"image":"mongo:6.0.5","name":"mongod","ports":[{"containerPort":27017,"name":"mongodb","protocol":"TCP"}],"resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"1","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"0.5","memory":"512Mi"}},"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/data/db","name":"data-volume"},{"mountPath":"/etc/mongo-keyfile","name":"mongo-keyfile","readOnly":true}]}],"securityContext":{"runAsGroup":0,"runAsUser":0},"volumes":[{"name":"mongo-keyfile","secret":{"defaultMode":256,"secretName":"mongo-keyfile"}}]}},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data-volume"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"10Gi"}}}}]}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T00:24:17Z"
  generation: 2
  name: mongodb-replica
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "7769"
  uid: b411433a-4824-4469-919b-342901d7e4c5
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-replica
  serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet
        - mongodb-replica
        - --bind_ip_all
        - --auth
        - --keyFile=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile
        image: mongo:6.0.5
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: mongod
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
          name: mongodb
          protocol: TCP
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /data/db
          name: data-volume
        - mountPath: /etc/mongo-keyfile
          name: mongo-keyfile
          readOnly: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext:
        runAsGroup: 0
        runAsUser: 0
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - name: mongo-keyfile
        secret:
          defaultMode: 256
          secretName: mongo-keyfile
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: data-volume
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending
status:
  availableReplicas: 3
  collisionCount: 0
  currentReplicas: 2
  currentRevision: mongodb-replica-77f75f4f95
  observedGeneration: 2
  readyReplicas: 3
  replicas: 3
  updateRevision: mongodb-replica-77f75f4f95
  updatedReplicas: 2

input.command: kubectl patch statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb --type merge -p '{"spec":{"replicas":3}}'; sleep 3; kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb; kubectl get pods -n mongodb

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  echo "== $i $(date +%T) =="
  kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='desired={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas}{"\n"}'
  kubectl get pods -n mongodb --no-headers
  sleep 5
done

content.text: Both new pods (3, 4) are running and stable. Now let's add them to the replica set.
Both members were added successfully. Let's verify the replica set status and check StatefulSet stability again (mindful of the external process).

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p 'admin123password' --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "rs.status().members.map(m=>({id:m._id,name:m.name,state:m.stateStr,health:m.health}))" 2>&1
echo "---statefulset---"
kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='desired={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas}{"\n"}'

content.text: 5 members registered, new ones in STARTUP2 (initial sync). Let me wait for them to become SECONDARY and keep monitoring the StatefulSet for the external process interference.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  echo "== $i $(date +%T) =="
  kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='desired={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas}{"\n"}'
  kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p 'admin123password' --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "rs.status().members.map(m=>m._id+':'+m.stateStr).join(' ')" 2>&1
  sleep 10
done

description:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  echo "== $i $(date +%T) =="
  kubectl get statefulset mongodb-replica -n mongodb -o jsonpath='desired={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas}{"\n"}'
  kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p 'admin123password' --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "rs.status().members.map(m=>m._id+':'+m.stateStr).join(' ')" 2>&1
  sleep 10
done
